title: Add a test suite
description: There are zero automated tests. At minimum add pytest tests covering: all db/queries.py public functions (using a fresh in-memory SQLite DB per test), report_jobs() filter combinations, search_all() with SHA-256 branch, backfill_metadata_keys() merge logic, and record_delivery() cascade. The queries module is already pure-function-friendly and the only dependency is get_conn(), which is easy to monkeypatch.

title: Remove or consolidate duplicate modules
description: Several modules are near-identical duplicates that create import confusion and maintenance burden: (1) accessibility_mgr/services/event_stream.py and event_streaming.py both define EventStreamService with slightly different APIs. (2) accessibility_mgr/security/authentication.py and accessibility_mgr/services/authentication.py both define AuthenticationService. (3) accessibility_mgr/services/analytics.py and persistent_analytics.py are referenced but only one is used in the UI. Pick one version of each pair, delete the other, and update all imports.

title: Fix missing persistent_analytics and persistent_provenance modules
description: accessibility_mgr/api/rest_api.py imports PersistentAnalyticsService from ..services.persistent_analytics and PersistentProvenanceRegistry from ..services.persistent_provenance, but neither file appears in the repository. The API layer will fail to import. Either create stub implementations consistent with the in-memory versions in analytics.py and provenance_registry.py, or update rest_api.py to import those existing classes directly.

title: Fix missing db/database.py referenced by SQLAlchemy models
description: accessibility_mgr/models/assets.py and models/inventory.py both import from ..db.database (Base, etc.), but database.py is not present in the repository. Since the SQLAlchemy models are described as legacy/compatibility shims, either: (a) add a minimal database.py that declares a SQLAlchemy Base, or (b) remove the models/ package entirely and delete the models import alias in __init__.py. Leaving it as-is causes an ImportError on any code path that touches the models package.

title: Replace manual _migrate() with a versioned migration system
description: schema.py uses a hand-rolled _migrate() function that checks for missing columns with PRAGMA table_info(). This is brittle: it cannot handle column renames, index changes, or multi-step dependent migrations, and there is no migration history table. Replace with Alembic (already in requirements.txt) or a simple integer-versioned migration table. At minimum, record an applied-migrations table so migrations are idempotent without PRAGMA column scans.

title: Make the database path configurable
description: DB_PATH is hardcoded as Path(__file__).parent.parent / 'accessibility_manager.db', placing the live database inside the installed package directory. This makes multi-instance use impossible, complicates packaging, and means 'uv sync' could overwrite the DB on reinstall. Read the path from an environment variable (e.g. ACCESSMAN_DB_PATH) or a config file, defaulting to a user data directory such as platformdirs.user_data_dir().

title: Extract shared job-detail components to eliminate repeated code
description: The metadata dialog, file-ingest dialog, event-log renderer, and step-completion logic are copy-pasted across braille_jobs.py, lp_ebraille.py, tactile_graphics.py, and print_jobs.py with only minor parameter differences. Extract these into shared helper functions in ui/components.py or a new ui/job_components.py. This will reduce the UI layer by roughly 1,000 lines and make future changes apply consistently across all job types.

title: Add pagination or virtual scrolling to all list views
description: Every list view (braille jobs, print jobs, inventory, file registry, event log) fetches all rows from SQLite and renders them all in the DOM. A production database with hundreds of jobs will cause slow page loads and browser memory issues. Add server-side pagination (page size 50 is a reasonable default) with prev/next controls, or use NiceGUI's virtual scroll. The search_all() and report_jobs() functions already accept a limit parameter — wire that through the UI.

title: Add date-input validation across all job forms
description: Request date, due date, and delivery date fields are plain text inputs with placeholder text 'YYYY-MM-DD'. Nothing prevents a user from entering 'next tuesday' or '13/01/2026', which silently stores invalid data and breaks report date-range filters. Add a date-picker widget (NiceGUI has ui.date) or at minimum validate with a regex/datetime.date.fromisoformat() check before saving, and show an inline error.

title: Wire unused services to the UI or remove them
description: The following services are fully implemented but have no UI exposure and are never called from app.py: RBACService, AnalyticsService (operational KPIs), SLAMonitoringService, WorkflowDAGService, MultiTenantService, ArtifactRetentionService, AuditLogService, DistributedWorkerRegistry, EventStreamService, and ComplianceReportingService. Decide which are genuinely needed for the current single-user local application, expose them in Admin or a new Operations page, and delete or clearly mark as future work the rest.

title: Implement the structural_map_node table in the UI
description: The database schema, all INSERT/SELECT helpers, and the __all__ export for structural_map_node exist in queries.py, but no UI page uses them. This table is intended to capture the METS-style structural map (book sections, chapters, etc.) for preservation metadata. Add a collapsible structural map section to the job detail view or the ingestion page that allows operators to add parent/child section nodes linked to specific files.

title: Make Dashboard stat cards clickable navigation targets
description: The Dashboard renders stat cards for job counts, in-progress items, low stock, and urgent jobs, but clicking them does nothing. Each card should navigate to the corresponding filtered page: the 'Urgent' card should open the Reports page pre-filtered to priority=urgent; 'Low Stock' should open Filament or Paper inventory; each job-type card should open that job type's list. This is a high-value UX improvement with minimal code.

title: Add a per-job PDF or print-ready summary export
description: Operators need to produce a paper record or summary document for each job (e.g., a delivery receipt, a production summary for a student IEP file). Add an 'Export Summary' button on job detail views that generates a simple HTML or PDF containing: job metadata, all Dublin Core fields, workflow step completion dates (from the event log), delivery details, and attached file checksums. The backup_service pattern of running in a thread is a good model.

title: Expose the REST API with configurable authentication
description: accessibility_mgr/api/platform_api.py defines a FastAPI app with /health, /workflows, /analytics, and /provenance endpoints, but it is never mounted or started. The AccessibilityPlatformAPI in rest_api.py is a service facade that already wraps the real DB-backed services. Mount the FastAPI app alongside NiceGUI using NiceGUI's app.mount() so external scripts or integrations can query job data. Add an API-key header check using the existing AuthenticationService.

title: Guard against concurrent-write races in the backup scheduler
description: BackupService._scheduled_run() reschedules the next timer inside a finally block, which is correct, but run_backup() opens a source SQLite connection and a destination connection without acquiring the application-level WAL checkpoint lock. If a large write transaction is active during backup, the backup() call may produce a partially-checkpointed file. Wrap run_backup() in a threading.Lock shared with the main get_conn() context manager, or use VACUUM INTO which is atomic with respect to WAL.

title: Move noqa: S608 suppressions to proper parameterised queries
description: Several functions in queries.py build SQL strings with f-strings and suppress S608 (possible SQL injection) with inline noqa comments: list_students(), list_material_categories(), list_workflow_steps(), complete_step(), and revert_step(). The table and column names come from controlled internal dictionaries, so the risk is low, but _SAFE_TABLES and _STEP_TABLES should be explicitly validated before interpolation (which they are), and this should be documented in a comment rather than silenced with noqa.

title: Add a bulk-action mode to job list views
description: There is no way to delete, export, or change the priority of multiple jobs at once. Add checkboxes to each row in the list views and a floating action bar that appears when any checkbox is selected, offering: delete selected, export selected (CSV), and mark all selected as delivered. This is especially useful for end-of-semester batch operations.

title: Add keyboard shortcut support and quick-create from Dashboard
description: Power users creating many jobs per day have no keyboard shortcuts. Add: (1) a global '+' floating action button on the Dashboard linking to a quick-create dialog that lets the user pick job type and fill in the minimum required fields; (2) press 'n' on a job list page to open the new-job dialog; (3) press Escape to close any open dialog. NiceGUI supports keyboard event listeners via ui.keyboard.

title: Add a tools.ini.example file and document configuration
description: The tools_service.py module reads tools.ini from the project root and warns when it is absent, but no example file exists in the repository. Users have no reference for valid keys. Create tools.ini.example with all four tool keys (ace, epubcheck, pipeline, liblouis) and a commented [paths] extra section, and add it to .gitignore exclusions so the live tools.ini is not committed.

title: Persist sidebar scroll position and active page across full page refreshes
description: On a full browser reload, the application always renders the Dashboard, losing the user's place. NiceGUI supports storing small values in browser storage via app.storage.user. Save the last active page name and restore it on load so a refresh in the middle of editing a braille job returns to the Braille Jobs list.

title: Add due-date overdue highlighting and a sorted 'Upcoming Deadlines' widget to Dashboard
description: Job due dates are stored and displayed but never compared to today's date. Add: (1) red text or a warning badge on any job card where due_date < today and delivered == 0; (2) a 'Upcoming Deadlines' card on the Dashboard showing the next 10 undelivered jobs sorted by due_date ascending, across all job types. The report_jobs() function already returns due_date and status, so this is mostly a UI change.

title: Add step completion timestamps to the job tables
description: Workflow steps are stored as integer 0/1 flags. There is no record of when each step was completed. Add a corresponding _date column for each step (e.g., digitized_date, formatted_date) that is set to datetime('now') when complete_step() is called and cleared to NULL when revert_step() is called. This enables cycle-time reporting (how long does formatting take on average?) and is necessary for accurate SLA monitoring.

title: Add a full-text search index to speed up search_all()
description: search_all() executes up to eight separate LIKE '%term%' queries, each doing a full table scan. On a database with thousands of jobs this will be slow. Add FTS5 virtual tables (SQLite's built-in full-text search) for the text columns of each job table, file_object, job_metadata, and metadata_event. The search_all() function can then use the FTS index for the primary match and fall back to LIKE only for checksum matching.

title: Separate lp_ebraille, epub3_daisy, and ebraille into distinct job types
description: All three job types (large_print, ebraille, epub3_daisy) share the lp_ebraille_job table and are distinguished only by a job_type text column. This works but makes per-type reporting queries more complex and means report_jobs() groups them all under 'lp_ebraille'. Consider either: (a) adding display-name aliases in the UI layer (cheap), or (b) splitting into three tables with appropriate indexes (thorough). Option (a) is sufficient if the current table structure is intentional.

title: Remove excessive boilerplate docstrings from auto-generated stubs
description: Many functions have machine-generated docstrings that add noise without information, e.g.: '""" do. Returns ------- Any Function result. """' on a private lambda or '""" rm. Parameters ---------- r : Any r parameter."""' on a one-line remove callback. These should either be removed entirely or replaced with a single meaningful sentence. The pattern appears in inventory_panels.py, seed_import.py, production.py, and auth_service.py.
